EigenEngine Êi|gen⟩ ⊗ Ên|gen⟩

An engine that compiles a working corpus into a typed concept graph, and the graph into a planned, gated, evidence-linked manuscript.

What it is

A document engine. The corpus — notes, sources, numerics — is compiled into a conceptric: a graded graph of typed nodes (claims, definitions, results, limitations) and their relations. A manuscript is planned over that graph as a storyboard, projected into LaTeX, and driven through rounds of mechanical review before a version is minted.

conceptric_v1.json · nodes / edges · sections + appendices planned

What distinguishes it

The separation of writing and judging, enforced in code. Builders never score their own output; review findings are typed records carrying evidence; a gap stays open rather than being smoothed. The convergence loop refuses to mint while any hard gate fails — the current falqon build is refused, and the refusal is drawn in this page's converge stage below.

rounds/harnessed · open findings at round · hard gates failing

Why that matters

The output can be audited instead of trusted. Of the falqon manuscript's 148 rendered objects, 122 trace back to the concept they argue, and all 54 equations resolve through their derivations; the 26 objects that do not yet resolve are listed in an unresolved band with their reasons.

lineage index · 91 concepts · 62 realised into the paper

Walk the interface

Enter the demo →

Four of the engine's real working surfaces, baked with the falqon manuscript's real data and served as static files — the same HTML and modules the local instrument runs. A tour bar carries you through in production order; click around freely at every station.

  1. The conceptric — the concept graph, drawn by its links; descend into any node's content and neighbourhood.
  2. The storyboard — the narrative plan over the graph, with coverage: which claims are placed, which are not.
  3. The artefact — the compiled candidate PDF with its margin of review findings keyed to source.
  4. The lineage — the joins: paragraph → section → concept → corpus, unresolved links shown as their own band.
read-only — author verbs are refused at the door · no account, nothing recorded · baked at commit ()
The machine

One pipeline, five stages

Select a stage — it opens in place. Every count on this band is the falqon manuscript's, generated from the engine tree at commit .

Everything begins as ordinary files: manuscript sources, numerics with their readings files, feedback ledgers, prior drafts. No worker reads the pile flat — a dispatched agent is pointed at one node of the structure and a goal, and reconstructs the context it needs from there. The dispatch layer enforces the rule on every dispatched prompt.

Dispatched workers also run at or below the orchestrator's own model tier, and an audit walks every workflow to check it, rather than trusting the author of the dispatch.

reconstruct(node, goal), never read(file) compile_dispatch · the dispatch kernel
the model ceiling, audited audit_dispatch_models.py

The compiler types every node and grades the graph, so coarse structure sits above fine detail. This is the falqon manuscript's real graph. Hover a node for its label and edges — the six definitions no claim consumes are ringed red: a connectivity gate caught them, and they shipped as they are.

hover a node — tap on a phone
typed nodes, graded descent nodes · edges · kinds:
shown refusal: six def-orphans kept connectivity gate · def_orphans · all six still in the shipped graph
walk this graph in the demo →

The plan is itself a data object: sections and appendices, each drawing on named graph nodes, with coverage tracked — which claims are placed, which are still homeless. Re-planning verbs (retitle a section, move a beat, re-spine the order) are writes through an actor-guarded door: a record arriving without an actor, or dressed as someone it is not, is refused and kept for inspection.

the plan over the graph projection_plan.json · coverage per section
shown refusal: forged actors bounce the actor guard · refused, kept for inspection
open the real board in the demo →

Every compiled build is read by a panel of detector classes — meta-leak, captions, apparatus, claims, house style, visuals, template, promises. Findings are typed records; the drain that consumes them validates evidence before counting anything closed, and has refused the orchestrator's own claimed closures. The loop refuses to lock while any hard gate fails.

Findings rise across these rounds because the detector suite widens as it learns the manuscript. The falqon build is still refused: the loop will not mint a release from it in this state.

builder and judge, separated blinded review passes · typed findings
shown refusal: unevidenced closures bouncedthe friction drain · evidence-or-open

A minted candidate is an addressed object: , carrying findings on its ledger ( hard · soft · deferred) and banked revisions. Every paragraph joins backward — paragraph → storyboard section → concept → corpus. The band below weights each section of the paper by how much of the manuscript realises in it; the unresolved objects sit in their own band at the end.

the same band as a table
the joins, queryable 148 objects · 122 trace their origin · 54/54 equations resolve · 26 unresolved, listed
read the candidate PDF in the demo → walk the joins in the demo →
Artifacts of record

What the engine has shipped

The engine's code is private; its outputs are public — the papers, the talks, this page and the demo.

  • The EigenEngine paper and talk — one object, two projections: a manuscript and a slide deck, each generated by the engine and pointing at the other.
    paper → talk → EigenEngine.pdf · eigenengine-talk.pdf
  • The falqon manuscript, worked in anger — a physics paper built through the full pipeline; its graph, plan, candidate PDF and lineage are the demo above.
    the demo → 62-claim curation · numerics/*_readings.json ·
  • The synthetics working drafts — the theory the engine is built from, five papers presented foundation-first.
    the result ladder → synthetics.html · /assets/docs
  • Talks with their lineage kept — the Laws of Learning deck banked as v1 (agent draft) → v2 (hand-tuned) → v3 (author-tuned); Spanspermia generated from a spoken opening.
    the talks → laws-of-learning v1→v3 · spanspermia
Provenance

How this page was made

the build record

This is a static page on a static host, so every liveness claim is bounded the same way: baked at a commit. The shell — head, navigation, footer, identity tokens — is generated by the engine's site backend from a spine file; this content region is authored; every number and both drawn exhibits come from one generated data file, emitted from the engine tree at commit (). A drift gate fails the build when the generated shell and its sources disagree; link, meta, de-slop and 375px visual gates run before every push. The demo bundle states its own bake commit in its banner.

shell: site_backend · spine: site/spine.json · innards: site/content/eigenengine.html · data: assets/js/eigenengine-page-data.js (scripts/site_assets/build_eigenengine_page_data.py) · demo: scripts/export_demo.py
